purpose: Each mutant is a minimal self-contained gameboy-doctor-format trace encoding ONE injected defect at exactly one line. `bin/falsify emulator-gb` runs the trace-diff gate against golden (expect PASS) then every mutant (expect REJECT). Falsifiability score = mutants rejected / total, conditioned on golden passing; a P0 gate requires 1.0. A mutant whose corrupted line the trace-diff fails to flag proves the gate is a false-green and BLOCKS the build.
trace_format: A:NN F:NN B:NN C:NN D:NN E:NN H:NN L:NN SP:NNNN PC:NNNN PCMEM:b0,b1,b2,b3 (each NN one hex byte, NNNN a 16-bit hex word)

mutants.name: force-h-zero
mutants.defect: AND instruction sets half-carry H=0 instead of the mandatory H=1
mutants.invariant_violated: AND flag rule (Z N=0 H=1 C=0)
mutants.reject_contract: at PC:0103 reference F=0x20 (H=1, bit 5), mutant F=0x00 (H=0) -> trace-diff divergence

mutants.name: skip-f-mask
mutants.defect: F register low nibble not masked to 0 (Z80 leftover bits leak); F=0x1F instead of 0x10
mutants.invariant_violated: F &= 0xF0 after every write (DMG has no F bits 0-3)
mutants.reject_contract: at PC:0101 reference F=0x10, mutant F=0x1F -> trace-diff divergence (truth F low nibble is always 0)

mutants.name: jr-off-by-one
mutants.defect: JR relative jump target off by one (omits the +2 instruction-length advance before adding signed e)
mutants.reject_contract: after JR reference PC=0x0107 (PCMEM 00,..), mutant PC=0x0106 (PCMEM desync) -> trace-diff divergence; downstream trace fully desynchronizes
